cityDNA.coreFantasy: A troll fen-city where nursery basins, lock houses, archives, healing courts, training arenas, memory-tablet government, and a bridge-back hall make strength protective.
cityDNA.readerTakeaway: Grendhollow is protective before it is frightening; its size exists to shelter fragile things.
cityDNA.civilizationThesis: Grendhollow turns mud, young, water, memory, care, healing, and disciplined strength into a swamp civic order of guarded continuity.
cityDNA.visualIdentity: Broad wet streets, shell domes, reed pavilions, coral terraces, mossy stone, malachite civic detail, nursery pools, canal locks, archive towers, healing lanterns, and arena grounds sized for huge patient bodies.
cityDNA.culturalIdentity: A troll civilization of deliberate citizens whose status comes from nursery care, waterworks skill, memory keeping, healing trust, and disciplined strength.
cityDNA.symbolicIdentity: Nursery basins, shell inlay, memory tablets, lock wheels, canal reflections, healing lanterns, champion gates, mossy stone, and bridge-backs symbolize strength that carries others.

canonLocks: Grendhollow is a mature troll fen-city, not a crude monster settlement.
The Wonder is the Trollstone Bridge-Back Hall of the Fen.
The Beacon is the Nursery Basin Beacon.
The Seat of Power is the Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry.
The five primary districts are Nursery / Spawning Grounds, Canal / Riverworks, Records / Archives, Medical / Healing, and Arena / Training.

The uploaded district anchors include Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, Shell Inlay Monument, Spawning Pool Nook, Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, Lock House, Canal Reflections Monument, Waterwheel Court Nook, Records / Archives Hall, Archive Tower, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, Copyist Hall Nook, Medical / Healing Civic Hall, Healing Hall, Medical Healing Lantern Monument, Apothecary Court Nook, Arena / Training Civic Hall, Training Arena, Woven Banners Monument, and Champion Gate Nook.
provisionalCanon: Grendhollow's highest civic virtue is guardianship.
The central tension is protective slowness versus civic stagnation.
Arena strength is valuable only when it protects nursery, healing, memory, and water systems.
rejectedReads: Grendhollow is a savage swamp monster culture.
Arena strength is random violence rather than civic discipline.
Nursery and healing systems are side details rather than central civic identity.
cautionFlags: Use troll scale through broad thresholds, huge benches, heavy railings, and low halls built for weight.
Keep nursery and healing systems visible alongside archives and arena training.
Water, mud, moss, and swamp routing are civic infrastructure, not background muck.
Preserve exact care, riverworks, archive, healing, and arena sidecar anchor names.

environment.environmentalLogic: The fen is nursery, road, danger, memory surface, and civic foundation. Grendhollow builds around swamp water rather than draining it away.

skyline: Trollstone Bridge-Back Hall of the Fen as massive wonder anchor
Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry as central authority
Nursery Basin Beacon as signal heart
Archive Tower, Healing Hall, Lock House, and arena gates distributed through mist and wet stone

civilization.people: Citizens are trolls: huge, slow-moving, physically powerful, communal, protective of young, tied to old stone memory, and deliberate rather than stupid or chaotic.
civilization.governance: Authority is memory-registry based and protective: the Colossus Registry records names, care duties, water obligations, healing claims, and civic continuity across generations.
civilization.religion: Religious expression is embedded in nursery protection, ancestral memory tablets, bridge-back endurance, healing rites, and ceremonies that honor strength used to carry others.

streets.appearance: Wet, heavy, mossed, slow, and protective: reed shade, shell-lit nursery basins, lock-house bridges, archive steps, healing gardens, training mud, and huge civic thresholds.
streets.movement: Movement follows broad bridges, wet steps, heavy ramps, canal crossings, lock paths, archive stairs, nursery edges, healing courts, and arena gates built for troll bodies.
streets.lighting: Blue nursery light, warm healing lanterns, swamp reflection, and fog-softened daylight make the city feel sheltered rather than bright.
streets.weatherEffects: Fog, rain, and high water reshape routes, deepen mud, slow movement, and make locks, basins, and raised crossings essential.
streets.dayCycle: Mornings begin with nursery tending, lock checks, archive opening, healing rounds, and water-level reading.
streets.nightCycle: Night is quiet pools, guarded nursery light, warm healing halls, archive lamps, lock-house watch, and distant arena training.

housing.wealthy: Registry- and healing-adjacent heavy stone homes with broad halls, private pools, memory tablet alcoves, malachite detail, and protected courtyards.
housing.middle: Canal-family houses, lock-worker homes, archive apartments, healer rooms, reed porches, wet steps, and large shared hearths.
housing.poor: Fen-edge dwellings, low mossy rooms, shared water access, reed screens, mud floors, raised storage, and communal shelter near work routes.

districts.name: Nursery / Spawning Grounds
districts.civicPurpose: Protected young, warm pools, spawning rites, family continuity, and the city’s emotional signal heart.
districts.visualIdentity: Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, Nursery Basin Beacon, Shell Inlay Monument, Spawning Pool Nook, warm water, low protective walls, caregivers, and blue light.

districts.name: Canal / Riverworks
districts.civicPurpose: Water control, swamp routing, flood response, lock scheduling, and practical movement.
districts.visualIdentity: Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, Lock House, Canal Reflections Monument, Waterwheel Court Nook, wet masonry, broad bridges, sluices, and lock workers.
districts.emotionalIdentity: Practical, damp, patient, and mechanically calm.

districts.name: Records / Archives
districts.civicPurpose: Troll memory, copied names, public continuity, legal obligation, and recorded care duties.
districts.visualIdentity: Records / Archives Hall, Archive Tower, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, Copyist Hall Nook, memory tablets, mossed archive stairs, and blue lamps.

districts.name: Medical / Healing
districts.civicPurpose: Recovery, public care, triage, herbal knowledge, and healing trust.
districts.visualIdentity: Medical / Healing Civic Hall, Healing Hall, Medical Healing Lantern Monument, Apothecary Court Nook, herb gardens, warm lamps, broad treatment benches, and quiet water.

districts.name: Arena / Training
districts.civicPurpose: Disciplined strength, readiness, public tests, controlled conflict, and champion memory.
districts.visualIdentity: Arena / Training Civic Hall, Training Arena, Woven Banners Monument, Champion Gate Nook, muddy grounds, massive training posts, and spectators on broad terraces.

wonder.name: Trollstone Bridge-Back Hall of the Fen
wonder.symbolicRole: Strength as carrying others across danger.
wonder.visualRole: Massive bridge-back hall of trollstone, moss, wet stone, broad arches, and fen-facing terraces rising like an old troll who refused to fall.
wonder.civicRole: Wonder-scale gathering site for endurance rites, public memory, citywide shelter, and ceremonies of guardianship.
storyHooks: A bridge-back oath requires a strong citizen to carry a vulnerable enemy to safety.

beacon.name: Nursery Basin Beacon
beacon.symbolicRole: Protection of young as the city's signal heart.
beacon.visualRole: Blue-lit beacon rising beside shell-lined nursery basins, warm pools, protective walls, and caregivers.
beacon.civicRole: Coordinates nursery keepers, lock crews, archivists, healers, arena trainers, and emergency response around vulnerable life.
storyHooks: A Beacon change turns every civic institution toward the nursery first.

seatOfPower.name: Memory Tablet Walls Colossus Registry
seatOfPower.symbolicRole: Memory, names, duty, and continuity made official law.
seatOfPower.visualRole: Huge registry complex of memory tablet walls, broad troll-scale halls, carved names, mossed stone, blue lamps, and formal record terraces.
seatOfPower.civicRole: Government center for records, care obligations, water duties, healing claims, arena honors, and generational memory.
storyHooks: A name on the wall assigns a duty nobody alive remembers accepting.

majorLandmarks.name: Lock House
majorLandmarks.symbolicRole: Water controlled without denying the swamp.
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Wet masonry lock house, sluices, broad bridges, waterwheels, canal reflections, and heavy doors.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Water routing, lock schedules, flood control, and canal passage.
storyHooks: A lock delay reveals whether the city protects memory or lives first.

majorLandmarks.name: Archive Tower
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Mossed archive tower with gold seals, copyist halls, memory tablets, and heavy stairways.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Stores records, names, care assignments, and historical duties.
storyHooks: A copied name changes who must guard the nursery.

majorLandmarks.name: Healing Hall
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Warm healing hall with lantern monument, herb beds, broad benches, apothecary court, and calm water.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Medical care, triage, herbal treatment, and public healing trust.
storyHooks: A healing record proves a wound was protected rather than hidden.

majorLandmarks.name: Training Arena
majorLandmarks.visualRole: Muddy arena, woven banners, champion gate, huge practice posts, broad terraces, and heavy stone markers.
majorLandmarks.civicRole: Training, public tests, readiness, controlled conflict, and champion recognition.
storyHooks: A champion must prove restraint, not power.

districtDioramaGuidance: S1 must show Nursery / Spawning Grounds Civic Hall, Nursery Basin Beacon, Shell Inlay Monument, Spawning Pool Nook, warm pools, caregivers, and protective walls.
S2 must show Canal / Riverworks Civic Hall, Lock House, Canal Reflections Monument, Waterwheel Court Nook, sluice gates, lock workers, and broad bridges.
S3 must show Records / Archives Hall, Archive Tower, Records Archives Gold-Seal Monument, Copyist Hall Nook, memory tablets, archive stairs, and copyists.
S4 must show Medical / Healing Civic Hall, Healing Hall, Medical Healing Lantern Monument, Apothecary Court Nook, healers, herb beds, and treatment benches.
S5 must show Arena / Training Civic Hall, Training Arena, Woven Banners Monument, Champion Gate Nook, arena trainers, mud ground, and huge practice posts.
mapBackgroundGuidance: Show a large swamp troll city with nursery basin signal core, canal riverworks, archive tower, healing courts, arena grounds, Colossus Registry, and Trollstone Bridge-Back Hall.
Use troll scale and protected care systems together: broad thresholds, huge benches, heavy bridges, nursery pools, healing lanterns, and memory tablets.
requiredVisualElements: Trollstone Bridge-Back Hall of the Fen
elementsToAvoid: water and mud as background muck only
